---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size60[
1. Prepare for Fusion Day

2. Final Paper Work
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. ...



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Fusion Day Panel

.size40[
- Five certificates present

- Each certificate:
    - 4-6 certificate students
    - Ten minutes (out of 50?)

- Audience of FUSE 102 students 
]

???

I'll be in the audience but can't help manage




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Fusion Day Panel: Our Plan

.size40[
- 4 students, each gives a 2 minute elevator pitch
    - What's the problem?
    - Why important to you?
    - What are you proposing?
    - Any connections to the certificate classes?

- Final 2 minutes for any questions from the audience
]

???

### Volunteers?

<br>

*Split class into four groups, one per volunteer*

15 minutes, groups help our volunteer prep their 2 minute pitch

<br>

Let's hear each!

Constructive feedback?

<br>

SLIDE: Ok, let's get to work on paper 3!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 3
.size40[
Propose a policy to address your selected environmental problem that balances the interests of the relevant stakeholders against the constraints of established institutions and uncertainty.

- Support all claims with evidence (e.g. academic literature, real-world examples, etc.)
]

???



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/10-1-four_brains.jpg')
background-size: 40%
background-position: right
class: middle, slidegreen

.pull-left[
.center[
.size65[**Paper 3 should be a complete, stand-alone policy proposal**]
]]

???

Do NOT assume the reader is familiar with your first two papers or has a deep understanding of the problem itself.

<br>

You should be able to give your paper to a person interested in your problem and they should be able to understand why solving the problem is complicated and be convinced that your policy is a good idea.

This means you should DEFINITELY take the best bits from your first two papers and add them to this one.

### Does that make sense?

<br>

SLIDE: Ok, let's get more specific


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size40[
A strong policy proposal is:

- Built on a clear and compelling problem definition,

- Identifies and appeals to specific stakeholders,

- Is adapted to the relevant complicating factors, and

- Offers guidance for measuring the effectiveness of the policy over time.
]

???

The bottom line here is I'm looking for you to connect your ideas in very concrete ways to the material we've been studying all semester.
- Problem framings matter,
- Generating stakeholder buy-in matters,
- Engaging substantively with the complications matters, and
- Planning for measuring success matters.

<br>

### Bottom line: I'm **much** more interested in **small, specific proposals** that are adapted to the real obstacles you face rather than broad and impossible reconstructions of society.

- My expectation is not that you've designed the ultimate plan that makes everyone happy.
    - That's not a thing that exists in the real world.

- My expectation is that you propose a policy that explicitly considers and tries to navigate past these complications.

<br>

### Does that make sense?

### Any questions on the prompt or any of this advice




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 3
.size40[
Propose a policy to address your selected environmental problem that balances the interests of the relevant stakeholders against the constraints of established institutions and uncertainty.

- Support all claims with evidence (e.g. academic literature, real-world examples, etc.)
]

???
